rationale: With Novaris holding 28.9% of Tethyan territory and defender war fatigue rising, reinforcing key defensive positions prevents collapse and leverages recent battlefield resilience to stabilize the front.
The harassment of neutral shipping provides justification for a visible naval show of force to deter further incidents and signal control over critical waterways.
A precision strike against enemy supply nodes could disrupt their momentum and buy time for our forces to consolidate, exploiting their overstretched reinforcements noted in intelligence.
With crisis at 8.0 and over 28% territory lost, offensive sabotage of critical transport infrastructure degrades enemy mobilization and exploits their defensive war fatigue.
Amplify narratives of economic hardship and military stagnation to deepen war weariness and erode public support for continued resistance.
Disrupt coalition coordination by targeting shared intelligence platforms, taking advantage of their rapid reinforcement patterns revealed in recent battlefield shifts.
With sanctions at 43% and metals prices surging 19%, securing critical raw materials now is essential to sustain military production and offset long-term supply disruptions.
Given high international support (71%) for Tethys, unilateral economic measures are unlikely to gain coalition backing, but targeted denial of dual-use resources can still pressure their war economy.
To counter growing domestic war fatigue and fund prolonged operations, launch a national war bond campaign to decentralize fiscal burden and maintain public buy-in.
With Novaris holding 28.9% of territory and war fatigue eroding domestic support, reinforcing stabilized defensive lines at Krynychne prevents breakthroughs and buys time for strategic reassessment.
Harassment of neutral shipping risks escalation and supply interdiction; increased naval and air patrols deter further incidents while showcasing operational readiness.
Targeted strikes against forward logistics hubs disrupt Novaris’s ability to consolidate gains, exploiting their momentary pause after failed broad offensives.
At crisis level 8 and with over 28% territory lost, covert sabotage of Novaris rail networks and fuel depots directly degrades their operational tempo and counters their military advantage.
Disrupting Novaris command-and-control systems exploits their campaign recalibration, creating windows of confusion for our defensive repositioning.
Amplify narratives of Novaris battlefield failures and supply shortages to undermine domestic morale and increase political pressure on their leadership.
With over 28.9% territory under enemy control, establishing protected corridors demonstrates moral leadership, strengthens international support, and addresses growing humanitarian strain.
Leverage our 71% international support to formalize military-logistical coordination and secure expanded aid commitments amid rising war fatigue.
Deploy neutral monitors to investigate shipping harassment claims, de-escalate maritime tensions, and preserve freedom of navigation through a critical trade artery.
With metals prices surging 19% and sanctions at 43%, stockpiling critical war materiel now locks in supply advantages before inflation and scarcity worsen.
Coordinate with allied economies to tighten restrictions on dual-use exports, exploiting their weakened logistics and our strong coalition leverage.
Launch a domestic war bond campaign to offset rising military expenditures and counteract public war weariness by fostering civic participation in national defense.
